Turret XP
Playtest Docs Mod Portal Source

Factorio 2.0 mod

Turret XP

Install Veteran Cores in your favorite gun turrets, level them through combat, and shape them into snipers, bulwarks, brawlers, machine guns, or elemental hybrids.

Download Latest Release
Turret XP
Level 3 74 / 272 XP
HP400 / 400 Shooting speed10 + 15/s Range18 Ammopiercing x42 Est. DPS157.5/s Kills18 Damage dealt3,860

Current release

Version 0.10.4

2026-06-12

Changes

  • Replaced the prototype-backed Max HP augment with a scripted Shield core upgrade. Shield adds 50 shield per rank, absorbs incoming damage before turret HP, displays a blue in-world shield bar under the native HP bar while relevant, and starts recharging after a short delay without damage.
  • Removed the Range augment for now. Specialization and sub-specialization range modifiers remain prototype-backed.
  • Reduced the generated hidden turret prototype budget by removing Range/Max HP variant axes from body and bound-preview generation.
  • Added a prototype migration that collapses old Range/Max HP hidden bodies and old range-preview bound items/placeholders onto the new specialization-only prototypes.

Bugfixes

  • Retired saved Range and Max HP augment ranks during profile normalization so old Veteran Core and bound turret item tags no longer keep dead upgrade state.

What It Does

Veteran Cores

Only chosen turrets become unique. A core stores XP, upgrades, elements, name, and battle history, then moves by itself or bound to its turret body when the front line shifts.

Combat Growth

Turrets earn XP from damage and kill contribution. Points buy damage, shield, repair, lifesteal, crits, luck, double shots, XP gain, and bouncing bullets.

Specialized Defenders

Choose strong identities with tradeoffs: long-range snipers, rapid machine guns, tough bulwarks, or brutal close-range brawlers.

Element Projects

Pick fire, electric, explosive, or toxic effects, feed larger passive material ranks to strengthen them, and combine two elements on veteran turrets.

Playtest Path

Install from the in-game Mods interface, place a gun turret, install a Veteran Core, add ammo, and let it fight.

  1. Install or dev-create a Veteran Core.
  2. Let the turret shoot enemies.
  3. Check that XP and lifetime damage increase.
  4. Name the core, toggle its floating label, and move it to another turret or bind it for one-item turret moves.
  5. Run /turret-xp-dev to test level gates, complete passive element material ranks, then buy an upgrade or augment.
  6. Report confusing stats, pacing issues, or runtime errors.

Documentation

Architecture Runtime responsibilities, state, and ownership boundaries. Design Notes Gameplay, balance, UX, and compatibility direction. Progression Design Evolution, material gates, element ranks, combos, and long-term scaling.
